summary: No-ship: the slice hardcodes discipline skills in a way that can mis-route review work, and the new test does not actually verify the tiered mapping it claims to protect.

findings.title: T2 review is annotated with a UI-only skill for every deep task, not just UI work
findings.recommendation: Do not collapse every `review-by:*` value to `code-review + garrison-design-audit (UI)`. Either encode the UI condition explicitly outside the tier-only discipline table, or keep the generic review mapping here and add `garrison-design-audit` only when the task is actually UI. As written, the compiled routing section tells backend/security/migration work to satisfy review with a UI audit.

findings.title: The new vitest only checks skill-name presence, so tier regressions will still pass
findings.recommendation: Assert the exact discipline lines per tier, not just global substring presence. For example, verify that `T1-standard` contains `self-review → code-review` and does not contain `garrison-design-audit`/`run-garrison`/`garrison-governance`, and that `T2-deep` contains the expected review/testing/evidence/distribution mappings. Right now a router that moved every skill to the wrong tier would still satisfy this test.
